message:
chore(beads): update ledger for wa-oegrb.5.3 closure and wa-oegrb.5.4 assignment

Sync bead status changes from concurrent swarm activity:
- wa-oegrb.5.3 (vector retrieval storage pipeline): closed by
  MistyCompass after completing lifecycle integrity tests and fixing
  nondeterministic ranking assertion
- wa-oegrb.5.4 (hybrid rank fusion): now in_progress, assigned to
  MistyCompass continuing the semantic search track

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-29ib","title":"Implement step: start `cod login --device-auth` and parse device code","description":"# Task: Device auth prompt handling (start login + parse device code)

## Goal
Initiate device-auth login in the Codex pane and extract the one-time device code.

## Actions
- Send `cod login --device-auth\n`.

## Verification / parsing
- Use shared wait logic (`PaneWaiter`) to wait for the device-code prompt.
- Extract the code in the form `XXXX-YYYY` / `XXXX-YYYYY` (allow minor drift).
- Validate extracted code before passing to browser automation.

## Failure modes
- Device code prompt not found:
  - retry once with a short delay
  - if still missing, abort with actionable hint (what marker was expected, last tail hash)
- Policy denies input injection:
  - abort without retry, return “policy denied” reason

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - parser extracts valid codes from fixture transcripts
  - parser rejects malformed codes
  - timeout behavior produces stable error

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given fixture/simulated output, the device code is extracted reliably.
- If no device code appears within timeout, the step fails safely with actionable diagnostics and without logging raw pane content.

 {"id":"wa-29k1","title":"SnapshotEngine orchestrator — coordinate full mux state capture","description":"## Goal
Implement the central SnapshotEngine that orchestrates the full mux state capture process — coordinating layout capture, scrollback capture, process resolution, and agent session binding into a unified MuxSnapshot, then persisting it to SQLite.

## Background & Motivation
This is the "brain" of the snapshot system. It ties together all the individual capture components (schema from bd-cuz, storage from bd-nz6, scrollback from bd-ybq, layout from bd-2t2) into a single coherent workflow. Without this orchestrator, the individual components are useful but can't produce a complete, consistent snapshot.

The design draws inspiration from WezTerm's resurrect.wezterm plugin (which does periodic_save() in Lua) but implements it natively in Rust with wa's async runtime for much better performance and reliability. Unlike the Lua plugin which can only capture what the Lua API exposes, wa's SnapshotEngine has direct access to the mux protocol via the vendored DirectMuxClient.

## Technical Design

### Struct Definition
```rust
// Location: crates/wa-core/src/snapshot/engine.rs (new file)
pub struct SnapshotEngine {
    storage: Arc<StorageManager>,
    mux_client: Arc<DirectMuxClient>,  // or pool when bd-41w lands
    config: SnapshotConfig,
    last_snapshot_hash: RwLock<Option<String>>,
    in_progress: AtomicBool,
}

pub struct SnapshotConfig {
    pub enabled: bool,
    pub interval_seconds: u64,        // default: 300 (5 min)
    pub max_scrollback_lines: usize,  // default: 10000
    pub max_concurrent_captures: usize, // default: 10
    pub retention_count: usize,       // default: 10
    pub retention_days: u64,          // default: 7
}

pub enum SnapshotTrigger {
    Periodic,
    Manual,
    PreRestart,
    PreShutdown,
}
```

### Core Method: capture_snapshot()
```rust
pub async fn capture_snapshot(&self, trigger: SnapshotTrigger) -> Result<MuxSnapshot> {
    // 1. Check if another snapshot is in progress
    if self.in_progress.swap(true, Ordering::SeqCst) {
        return Err(Error::SnapshotInProgress);
    }
    let _guard = scopeguard::guard((), |_| self.in_progress.store(false, Ordering::Release));

    // 2. Capture layout tree (from bd-2t2)
    let layout = self.capture_layout().await?;
    
    // 3. Compute pane list hash — skip if unchanged since last snapshot
    let pane_hash = compute_pane_hash(&layout);
    if trigger == SnapshotTrigger::Periodic {
        if let Some(ref last) = *self.last_snapshot_hash.read().await {
            if *last == pane_hash {
                return Err(Error::NoChanges);
            }
        }
    }
    
    // 4. Capture scrollback for all panes in parallel (from bd-ybq)
    let scrollbacks = self.capture_scrollbacks_parallel(&layout).await?;
    
    // 5. Resolve agent sessions from wa's AgentSessionRecord table
    let agent_refs = self.resolve_agent_sessions(&layout).await?;
    
    // 6. Assemble MuxSnapshot (schema from bd-cuz)
    let snapshot = MuxSnapshot {
        version: 1,
        timestamp: Utc::now(),
        trigger,
        layout,
        scrollbacks,
        agent_refs,
        checksum: String::new(), // computed below
    };
    
    // 7. Compute and set checksum
    let checksum = compute_snapshot_checksum(&snapshot);
    
    // 8. Persist to SQLite (storage from bd-nz6)
    let snapshot_id = self.storage.save_snapshot(&snapshot).await?;
    
    // 9. Update last hash
    *self.last_snapshot_hash.write().await = Some(pane_hash);
    
    // 10. Run retention cleanup
    self.storage.cleanup_old_snapshots(&self.config).await?;
    
    Ok(snapshot)
}
```

### Periodic Snapshot Loop
Integrates with wa's existing tokio runtime:
```rust
pub async fn run_periodic(&self, mut shutdown: tokio::sync::watch::Receiver<bool>) {
    let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_secs(self.config.interval_seconds));
    loop {
        tokio::select! {
            _ = interval.tick() => {
                match self.capture_snapshot(SnapshotTrigger::Periodic).await {
                    Ok(snap) => info!("Periodic snapshot saved: {}", snap.id),
                    Err(Error::NoChanges) => debug!("No changes, skipping snapshot"),
                    Err(Error::SnapshotInProgress) => debug!("Snapshot already in progress"),
                    Err(e) => warn!("Periodic snapshot failed: {}", e),
                }
            }
            _ = shutdown.changed() => break,
        }
    }
}
```

### Configuration (wa.toml)
```toml
[snapshots]
enabled = true
interval_seconds = 300
max_scrollback_lines = 10000
max_concurrent_captures = 10
retention_count = 10
retention_days = 7
```

## Integration Points
- **StorageManager** (crates/wa-core/src/storage.rs): Add snapshot-related methods
- **DirectMuxClient** (crates/wa-core/src/vendored/mux_client.rs): Use for pane listing and scrollback
- **Config** (crates/wa-core/src/config.rs): Add SnapshotConfig section
- **Event bus**: Emit SnapshotCompleted events for wa's workflow engine
- **Watch daemon** (wa watch): Start periodic snapshot loop alongside capture loop

## Key Files to Create/Modify
- CREATE: crates/wa-core/src/snapshot/engine.rs
- CREATE: crates/wa-core/src/snapshot/mod.rs
- MODIFY: crates/wa-core/src/config.rs (add SnapshotConfig)
- MODIFY: crates/wa-core/src/storage.rs (add snapshot methods)
- MODIFY: crates/wa/src/main.rs (wire into watch daemon)

## Dependencies
- bd-cuz: MuxSnapshot schema (defines the data structures)
- bd-nz6: SQLite tables (provides persistence layer)
- bd-ybq: Scrollback capture (provides per-pane content extraction)
- bd-2t2: Layout tree capture (provides window/tab/pane hierarchy)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Periodic snapshots run at configured interval
- On-demand snapshots complete and return snapshot_id
- No-op detection works (skips when nothing changed since last snapshot)
- Concurrent scrollback capture respects max_concurrent_captures limit
- Agent session references are correctly populated from wa's session table
- Events emitted on snapshot completion for wa's workflow engine
- Retention cleanup runs after each snapshot (both count and age limits)
- Pre-restart snapshots block until complete (no timeout)
- Graceful degradation: partial failures (e.g., one pane's scrollback fails) don't abort the entire snapshot

## Estimated Effort
3-4 hours implementation, 1-2 hours testing

## macOS Compatibility
On macOS, process state inspection differs from Linux. Where the design references `/proc/<pid>/status` for resolving process state during snapshot capture, use `proc_pidinfo()` from `libproc` instead:
```rust
#[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
fn get_process_state(pid: i32) -> Result<ProcessState> {
    use libproc::libproc::proc_pid;
    let info = proc_pid::pidinfo::<proc_pid::BSDInfo>(pid, 0)?;
    // Map BSD process flags to our ProcessState enum
    Ok(ProcessState::from_bsd_flags(info.pbi_flags))
}

#[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
fn get_process_state(pid: i32) -> Result<ProcessState> {
    let status = std::fs::read_to_string(format!("/proc/{}/status", pid))?;
    // Parse "State:" line
    Ok(ProcessState::from_proc_status(&status))
}
```
Add `libproc` to Cargo.toml dependencies (macOS-only): `libproc = { version = "0.14", optional = true }`.

## Test Requirements

### Concurrency Testing (LabRuntime DPOR)
Use LabRuntime with Dynamic Partial Order Reduction to verify concurrent snapshot coordination:
- Verify that two concurrent `capture_snapshot()` calls never both proceed (the `in_progress` AtomicBool guard works correctly under all interleavings)
- Verify that periodic snapshot + manual snapshot don't corrupt shared state (`last_snapshot_hash` RwLock)
- Verify that concurrent scrollback captures (the parallel `capture_scrollbacks_parallel`) don't race on the DirectMuxClient connection pool
- Verify that snapshot + retention cleanup don't delete a snapshot that's still being written

### Criterion Benchmarks
Add benchmarks in `crates/wa-core/benches/snapshot_engine.rs`:
- `bench_snapshot_creation_200_panes`: Full snapshot of 200-pane mux state must complete in <100ms (excluding I/O)
- `bench_snapshot_no_change_detection`: Hash comparison for no-op detection, target <1ms
- `bench_snapshot_checksum`: Checksum computation for a 200-pane snapshot
- `bench_retention_cleanup`: Cleanup of 100 old snapshots from SQLite

### Property-Based Testing (proptest)
Add proptest cases in `crates/wa-core/tests/proptest_snapshot_engine.rs`:
- **State machine transitions**: Generate arbitrary sequences of `(SnapshotTrigger, MuxStateChange)` events and verify the engine never enters an inconsistent state (e.g., `in_progress` stuck true, `last_snapshot_hash` out of sync with actual last snapshot)
- **Config validation**: `arb_snapshot_config()` generates random configs; verify the engine accepts all valid configs and rejects invalid ones (zero interval, zero retention, etc.)
- **Partial failure resilience**: Generate random failure injection points during `capture_snapshot()`; verify the engine always resets `in_progress` and never leaves partial snapshots in storage

## Cross-References
- **wa-3kxe.3** (Differential snapshots): The `last_snapshot_hash` and no-op detection in this engine are the foundation for differential snapshots. wa-3kxe.3 extends this with fine-grained per-pane change tracking instead of the coarse whole-layout hash used here.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:31:26.999046Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:01:31.021039Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:01:31.021039Z","close_reason":"Implemented: snapshot_engine.rs with SnapshotEngine orchestrator, 12 passing tests. Captures topology + pane state + dedup hash, persists to SQLite, periodic loop with retention cleanup.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-29k1","depends_on_id":"wa-2t2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:35:14.503448Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-29k1","depends_on_id":"wa-3q7q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:35:14.341449Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-29k1","depends_on_id":"wa-cuz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:35:14.244907Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-29k1","depends_on_id":"wa-nz6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:35:14.341449Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-29k1","depends_on_id":"wa-ooje","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:35:14.503448Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-29k1","depends_on_id":"wa-rbvl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:35:14.244907Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-29k1","depends_on_id":"wa-rsaf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:34:57.878324Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-29k1","depends_on_id":"wa-vhbr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:35:14.424255Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-29k1","depends_on_id":"wa-ybq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T19:35:14.424255Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-2a4n","title":"Shell completions: static clap derivation + dynamic pane/workflow completion","description":"
# Shell Completions

## Purpose
Enable tab completion for all wa commands, including dynamic values like pane IDs and workflow names.

## Static Completions (clap derive)
```rust
#[derive(Parser)]
#[command(name = "wa")]
struct Cli {
    #[command(subcommand)]
    command: Commands,
}

// In main.rs
if let Some(shell) = args.completions {
    clap_complete::generate(shell, &mut cli, "wa", &mut io::stdout());
}
```

## Dynamic Completions
For pane IDs:
```bash
$ wa send --pane <TAB>
1 (codex @ /home/user/project)
3 (claude @ /home/user/other)
```

For workflow names:
```bash
$ wa workflow run <TAB>
handle_compaction    handle_usage_limits
```

## Implementation
Use clap_complete's custom completer:
```rust
fn complete_pane_id(current: &str) -> Vec<CompletionCandidate> {
    // Query running wa instance for pane list
    // Format as completion candidates
}
```

## Installation
```bash
# Bash
wa completions bash > ~/.bash_completion.d/wa

# Zsh
wa completions zsh > ~/.zfunc/_wa

# Fish
wa completions fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/wa.fish
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Static completions for all commands
- [ ] Dynamic completion for pane IDs
- [ ] Dynamic completion for workflow names
- [ ] Installation instructions in setup wizard
- [ ] Works in bash, zsh, fish

## Testing
- Unit tests for completion/alias generation and help formatting.
- Integration tests for help output snapshots and no-ANSI guarantees.
- CI smoke tests for completions and help stability.

-{"id":"wa-2abzy","title":"Replace tokio channels (mpsc/watch/broadcast) with asupersync","description":"# Replace tokio channels with asupersync

## Background
Channels are the most widespread async primitive in FrankenTerm. tokio channels are used for event passing, cancellation, state sync, and IPC.

## Scope
- tokio::sync::mpsc → asupersync::channel::mpsc (two-phase reserve/commit)
- tokio::sync::watch → asupersync::channel::watch
- tokio::sync::broadcast → asupersync::channel::broadcast

## Critical change: Two-phase send
asupersync channels use reserve/commit pattern:
```rust
// Before (tokio)
tx.send(msg).await?;  // Can lose msg if cancelled between enqueue and ack

// After (asupersync)
let permit = tx.reserve(cx).await?;  // Phase 1: reserve slot (cancellation safe)
permit.send(msg);                     // Phase 2: commit (infallible after reservation)
```

This eliminates the "message lost on cancellation" class of bugs but requires updating ALL channel send sites.

## Affected modules (high-impact)
- runtime.rs — 38+ mpsc uses for observation/command loops
- ipc.rs — 22+ uses for IPC message passing
- tailer.rs — capture event channels
- mcp.rs — MCP tool response channels
- mux_client.rs — pane output subscription channels
- native_events.rs — event listener channels

## Watch channel migration
Watch channels are used for cancellation signaling:
```rust
// Before
let (cancel_tx, cancel_rx) = tokio::sync::watch::channel(false);
// Signal cancellation
cancel_tx.send(true)?;
// Check cancellation
if *cancel_rx.borrow() { break; }

// After — consider using cx.checkpoint()? instead for cancellation
// or asupersync::channel::watch with Cx-aware API
```

## Acceptance criteria
- All tokio channel types replaced with asupersync equivalents
- Two-phase send pattern adopted at all send sites
- Watch-based cancellation migrated to cx.checkpoint() where appropriate
- No message loss under cancellation (the whole point!)
- Existing channel tests pass

## Concurrency verification
- **Loom model checking**: Use Loom to verify correctness of all replaced channel types:
  - MPSC: verify no message loss, no duplication, FIFO ordering under all interleavings
  - Watch: verify last-value semantics, no torn reads
  - Broadcast: verify all subscribers receive all messages, no message loss on slow consumers
  - Two-phase reserve/commit: verify that reserved permits are never leaked and that commit is truly infallible after successful reserve
  - Add `tests/loom_channels.rs` with separate `loom::model` blocks for each channel type.

## Benchmark requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks for channel throughput**: Add `benches/channel_throughput.rs` measuring:
  - MPSC single-producer single-consumer throughput (messages/sec)
  - MPSC multi-producer single-consumer throughput (2, 4, 8 producers)
  - Watch update + read throughput
  - Broadcast fan-out throughput (2, 4, 8 subscribers)
  - Two-phase reserve/commit overhead vs tokio's direct send
  - Include tokio baseline groups for direct comparison.

## Property-based testing
- **Proptest for message ordering**: Use proptest to generate arbitrary sequences of sends across multiple producers and verify that per-producer FIFO ordering is preserved at the consumer. Generate random interleaving schedules and verify no reordering, duplication, or loss occurs.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"StormyOwl","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:48:24.270254Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-14T01:14:17.374233Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2abzy","depends_on_id":"wa-3d14m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:51:57.585586Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}
+{"id":"wa-2abzy","title":"Replace tokio channels (mpsc/watch/broadcast) with asupersync","description":"# Replace tokio channels with asupersync

## Background
Channels are the most widespread async primitive in FrankenTerm. tokio channels are used for event passing, cancellation, state sync, and IPC.

## Scope
- tokio::sync::mpsc → asupersync::channel::mpsc (two-phase reserve/commit)
- tokio::sync::watch → asupersync::channel::watch
- tokio::sync::broadcast → asupersync::channel::broadcast

## Critical change: Two-phase send
asupersync channels use reserve/commit pattern:
```rust
// Before (tokio)
tx.send(msg).await?;  // Can lose msg if cancelled between enqueue and ack

// After (asupersync)
let permit = tx.reserve(cx).await?;  // Phase 1: reserve slot (cancellation safe)
permit.send(msg);                     // Phase 2: commit (infallible after reservation)
```

This eliminates the "message lost on cancellation" class of bugs but requires updating ALL channel send sites.

## Affected modules (high-impact)
- runtime.rs — 38+ mpsc uses for observation/command loops
- ipc.rs — 22+ uses for IPC message passing
- tailer.rs — capture event channels
- mcp.rs — MCP tool response channels
- mux_client.rs — pane output subscription channels
- native_events.rs — event listener channels

## Watch channel migration
Watch channels are used for cancellation signaling:
```rust
// Before
let (cancel_tx, cancel_rx) = tokio::sync::watch::channel(false);
// Signal cancellation
cancel_tx.send(true)?;
// Check cancellation
if *cancel_rx.borrow() { break; }

// After — consider using cx.checkpoint()? instead for cancellation
// or asupersync::channel::watch with Cx-aware API
```

## Acceptance criteria
- All tokio channel types replaced with asupersync equivalents
- Two-phase send pattern adopted at all send sites
- Watch-based cancellation migrated to cx.checkpoint() where appropriate
- No message loss under cancellation (the whole point!)
- Existing channel tests pass

## Concurrency verification
- **Loom model checking**: Use Loom to verify correctness of all replaced channel types:
  - MPSC: verify no message loss, no duplication, FIFO ordering under all interleavings
  - Watch: verify last-value semantics, no torn reads
  - Broadcast: verify all subscribers receive all messages, no message loss on slow consumers
  - Two-phase reserve/commit: verify that reserved permits are never leaked and that commit is truly infallible after successful reserve
  - Add `tests/loom_channels.rs` with separate `loom::model` blocks for each channel type.

## Benchmark requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks for channel throughput**: Add `benches/channel_throughput.rs` measuring:
  - MPSC single-producer single-consumer throughput (messages/sec)
  - MPSC multi-producer single-consumer throughput (2, 4, 8 producers)
  - Watch update + read throughput
  - Broadcast fan-out throughput (2, 4, 8 subscribers)
  - Two-phase reserve/commit overhead vs tokio's direct send
  - Include tokio baseline groups for direct comparison.

## Property-based testing
- **Proptest for message ordering**: Use proptest to generate arbitrary sequences of sends across multiple producers and verify that per-producer FIFO ordering is preserved at the consumer. Generate random interleaving schedules and verify no reordering, duplication, or loss occurs.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"StormyOwl","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:48:24.270254Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-14T01:23:37.516261Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2abzy","depends_on_id":"wa-3d14m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:51:57.585586Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}],"comments":[{"id":159,"issue_id":"wa-2abzy","author":"StormyOwl","text":"Progress slice (StormyOwl, 2026-02-14): migrated crates/frankenterm-core/src/snapshot_engine.rs off direct tokio sync imports to runtime_compat aliases: mpsc/watch + compat Mutex/RwLock. Replaced all tokio::sync::watch::channel callsites in snapshot engine tests and scheduler loop to watch::channel/watch::Receiver. Validation: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-stormyowl-wa2abzy cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib ✅; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-stormyowl-wa2abzy cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib snapshot_engine -- --nocapture ✅ (37 snapshot_engine tests passed). Workspace-level gates currently blocked by pre-existing unrelated errors/warnings in resize_scheduler.rs, search/chunking.rs, runtime.rs, search/chunk_vector_store.rs, and tests/proptest_dataflow.rs when running all-target checks/clippy.","created_at":"2026-02-14T01:23:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-2ahu0","title":"Memory pressure engine — per-pane budgets, eviction, and compression","description":"# Memory Pressure Engine

## Skills: /extreme-software-optimization, /alien-artifact-coding

## Problem
200 agents × 500MB = 100GB baseline. Long-lived agents grow to 1-10GB+ each. Without pressure management, OOM killer strikes randomly, potentially killing the mux server itself.

## Solution

### 1. Per-pane memory tracking
- **Linux**: Sample /proc/[pid]/statm every 30s for each pane process tree
- **macOS**: Use proc_pidinfo(PROC_PIDTASKINFO) for per-process RSS, virtual, shared memory
- Track RSS, virtual, shared memory per pane
- Compute total memory pressure: sum(pane RSS) / available RAM

### 2. Memory pressure tiers
- **Linux**: Keyed to /proc/pressure/memory PSI metrics
- **macOS**: Keyed to vm_statistics64 (free + inactive pages ratio) and os_proc_available_memory()
- avg10 < 10% (or macOS equivalent): Green — no action
- avg10 10-20%: Yellow — compress idle pane scrollback, warn user
- avg10 20-40%: Orange — evict oldest idle pane scrollback to disk, pause captures
- avg10 > 40%: Red — kill largest idle pane, emergency scrollback eviction

### 3. Scrollback compression and eviction
- Compress scrollback for panes idle > 5 min (zstd, ~10:1 ratio)
- Evict scrollback to disk for panes idle > 30 min
- Restore transparently on pane focus

### 4. OOM score adjustment
- **Linux**: Set oom_score_adj to protect mux server (-1000)
- **macOS**: No OOM score equivalent; use Jetsam priority via memorystatus_control (if available) or advisory
- Set agent panes proportional to age: newer = lower score, older = higher score
- Ensures OOM killer targets oldest idle agents first

### 5. Formal memory bound (/alien-artifact-coding)
Prove that the mux server own memory usage is bounded by O(n) where n = pane count, independent of scrollback size. Use formal methods.

## Tests
- Simulate memory pressure ramp, verify tier transitions
- Measure scrollback compression ratio and latency
- Verify OOM score adj protects mux server (Linux)
- 200-pane memory stress test: no OOM with 128GB limit
- **Use criterion benchmarks for compression throughput**
- **Test both Linux and macOS code paths**

## Acceptance criteria
- Memory pressure monitored via PSI (Linux) / vm_statistics64 (macOS)
- Scrollback compression saves >80% memory for idle panes
- Disk eviction transparent to user
- Mux server never killed by OOM
- Memory bound formally verified","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:11:54.455402Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-11T04:39:25.878472Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T04:39:25.878472Z","close_reason":"Memory pressure engine implemented: MemoryPressureTier, MemoryAction, MemoryPressureMonitor with Linux /proc/meminfo and macOS vm_stat/sysctl. Per-pane memory tracking types. 21 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"wa-2apg5","title":"Zellij session management analysis — persistence, reconnect, resurrection","description":"# Zellij Session Management Analysis

## Why this matters
Zellij has built-in session persistence and resurrection — a user can detach and reattach, and sessions survive server restarts. FrankenTerm's session persistence epic (wa-rsaf) is building this from scratch. Understanding how Zellij solved it could save significant design effort.

## What to analyze

### Session lifecycle
- How are sessions created, listed, attached, detached, destroyed?
- What state is persisted and where (filesystem, database, memory)?
- What happens on unexpected server crash vs clean shutdown?

### Session resurrection
- Can Zellij restore sessions after a server restart?
- What state is lost vs preserved (scrollback? running processes? environment?)
- How does it handle process re-launch (or does it?)

### Multi-client attachment
- Can multiple clients attach to the same session simultaneously?
- How are conflicts handled (input routing, resize, permissions)?
- This is directly relevant to FrankenTerm's agent swarm use case

### Session serialization format
- What format does Zellij use to serialize session state?
- How does it handle layout topology (splits, tabs, floating panes)?
- Compare to our planned MuxSnapshot schema (bd-cuz)

## FrankenTerm implications
For each finding, document: "FrankenTerm could use this by..." with a concrete implementation sketch.

## Acceptance criteria
- Session lifecycle fully documented
- Resurrection mechanism analyzed (what survives, what doesn't)
- Multi-client behavior analyzed
- Serialization format documented
- 3+ actionable recommendations for FrankenTerm

## Cross-references
- **wa-rsaf** (session persistence epic): Findings from this analysis directly inform the design of FrankenTerm's session persistence system. Specific areas of impact include: session serialization format (compare with MuxSnapshot schema bd-cuz), resurrection strategy (what state to persist and how), and multi-client attachment semantics for agent swarms.

## Notes
- This is one of the highest-value Zellij analysis beads because FrankenTerm's session persistence (wa-rsaf) is being designed from scratch. Zellij's battle-tested approach to session resurrection can save significant design iteration.
- Pay special attention to what state Zellij does NOT persist — these are likely the hard problems where persistence has diminishing returns.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RusticSnow","created_at":"2026-02-10T15:53:42.044778Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-11T06:33:53.114172Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T06:33:53.114172Z","close_reason":"Delivered evidence/zellij/session-management.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-2apg5","depends_on_id":"wa-22o1q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T15:55:01.973841Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-2apg5","depends_on_id":"wa-okyhm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T15:54:56.544663Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}

 {"id":"wa-oegrb.5","title":"Semantic track: embeddings + vector retrieval + hybrid rank fusion","description":"## Track objective
Implement semantic retrieval and hybrid lexical+semantic ranking over recorder data, reusing validated patterns from cass/xf where appropriate.

## Why this track exists
Pure lexical search misses semantically similar incidents and prior solutions. Hybrid ranking materially improves operator and agent recall in incident/debug workflows.

## Deliverables
- Embedding/chunking strategy for recorder corpus
- Vector retrieval subsystem and ANN strategy
- Rank-fusion strategy combining lexical and semantic evidence
- Offline relevance evaluation corpus and metrics

## Acceptance criteria
- Hybrid ranking behavior is deterministic enough to test and tune.
- Semantic subsystem can degrade gracefully without breaking lexical search.","notes":"Semantic track progress (PurpleEagle): (1) completed schema integrity repair slice for segment_embeddings (SCHEMA_VERSION 23 canonical schema + migration repair + unembedded query fix + regression tests). (2) completed storage-level vector retrieval + hybrid fusion slice in crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs: added store_embedding_f32(), semantic_search(), hybrid_search_with_results(), deterministic fusion metadata structs (SemanticSearchHit/HybridSearchResult/HybridSearchBundle), cosine-based semantic ranking over segment_embeddings with pane/time filters, and lexical-safe hybrid fallback behavior when semantic lane is empty; added async tests storage_handle_semantic_search_ranks_and_respects_filters and storage_handle_hybrid_search_blends_lexical_and_semantic. Validation: rustfmt on storage.rs PASS; cargo check -p frankenterm-core --all-targets PASS (warnings only); targeted tests PASS; cargo fmt --check FAIL from unrelated pre-existing formatting drift in other files; cargo clippy --all-targets -D warnings FAIL from broad pre-existing repo lint debt unrelated to this slice.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"PurpleEagle","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.478129Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:33:46.118205Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"labels":["cass","hybrid","search","semantic","xf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.478129Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.792305Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.5.1","title":"Define embedding provider interface and model-governance strategy","description":"## Context
Semantic retrieval needs a model/provider strategy that balances quality, latency, cost, privacy, and offline operability. This must be explicit before implementation.

## Scope
Define embedding provider interface, model selection rubric, and deployment modes (local/remote) for recorder search.

## Deliverables
- Embedding abstraction API with pluggable providers
- Model selection matrix (quality/latency/cost/privacy tradeoffs)
- Normalization/versioning strategy for embedding vectors
- Operational policy for outages and fallback behavior

## Why this matters
Semantic quality depends as much on model governance as algorithm design. Unclear provider rules create unstable, expensive systems.

## Acceptance
- Provider interface supports swapping implementations without query-layer changes
- Selection rationale is documented and reproducible
- Fallback behavior is deterministic when embeddings unavailable","notes":"Claimed to evaluate and land semantic embedding provider governance contract for wa-prefixed bead parity.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleSnow","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.226779Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T08:53:30.615043Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T08:53:30.615043Z","close_reason":"Embedding provider governance/interface contract documented and validated in docs/flight-recorder/embedding-provider-governance-contract.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"labels":["architecture","epic","flight-recorder","track-semantic"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:47.006784Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:47.058303Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.964617Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.226779Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":41,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.1","author":"GentleSnow","text":"Validated existing governance contract at docs/flight-recorder/embedding-provider-governance-contract.md against bead deliverables: (1) pluggable provider abstraction API (EmbeddingProvider + Registry), (2) model quality/latency/cost/privacy selection matrix and governance policy, (3) normalization + immutable profile_id versioning contract, and (4) deterministic query/index fallback semantics when semantic path is unavailable. This satisfies acceptance requirements for wa-oegrb.5.1 and unblocks wa-oegrb.5.2/wa-oegrb.5.3.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:53:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.5.2","title":"Implement context-aware chunking/windowing policy for semantic embeddings","description":"## Context
Terminal streams are noisy and irregular. Effective semantic retrieval requires principled chunking/windowing, not naive line-level embeddings.

## Scope
Design and implement chunking policy that preserves context boundaries (session/pane/direction/time) while keeping embedding size manageable.

## Deliverables
- Chunking/windowing algorithm and tunable parameters
- Boundary rules for commands, outputs, errors, and prompt transitions
- Metadata linkage from chunk -> source offsets for replay/audit
- Tests on edge cases (very long outputs, tiny fragments, mixed directions)

## Why this matters
Bad chunking degrades semantic recall/precision and undermines trust in hybrid results.

## Acceptance
- Chunking policy yields reproducible chunks across reindex runs
- Chunk metadata fully traces back to source log offsets
- Edge-case tests demonstrate robust boundary handling","notes":"Claimed to deliver semantic chunking/windowing policy contract + edge-case validation plan and source-offset traceability rules.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberWolf","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.291502Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T21:41:39.974246Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T21:41:39.974189Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"labels":["epic","flight-recorder","implementation","track-semantic"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:47.108011Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.291502Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.573241Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":128,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.2","author":"AmberWolf","text":"2026-02-13 AmberWolf takeover: claiming stale in-progress bead. Implementing semantic chunking/windowing policy in code (deterministic chunk IDs, hard/soft boundaries, overlap, glue behavior, and offset traceability metadata) plus fixture-style edge-case tests.","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:18:37Z"},{"id":134,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.2","author":"AmberWolf","text":"Implemented semantic chunking/windowing policy in code:\n- Added new module: crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/chunking.rs\n- Wired exports in crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/mod.rs\n- Added edge-case tests: crates/frankenterm-core/tests/semantic_chunking_policy_tests.rs\n\nImplemented behavior includes deterministic ordering/chunk IDs, hard + soft boundaries, overlap metadata on soft splits, tiny-fragment glue rules (ingress+egress => mixed), and source-offset traceability metadata.\n\nValidation:\n- rustfmt --edition 2024 --check on touched files: PASS\n- cargo test/check/clippy currently blocked in this workspace by many concurrent/stalled cargo/build-script processes from other agents; retried with isolated CARGO_HOME/CARGO_TARGET_DIR and still encountered build-script stalls.\n\nNext step from my side: rerun targeted test/check/clippy once global cargo contention clears, then close bead if green.","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:37:11Z"}]}
-{"id":"wa-oegrb.5.3","title":"Build vector retrieval storage pipeline aligned with recorder/index lifecycle","description":"## Context
We need a durable vector retrieval layer integrated with recorder/index lifecycle, including updates, deletes/retention handling, and checkpoint alignment.

## Scope
Implement vector index storage/retrieval path and lifecycle management aligned with recorder offsets and lexical index updates.

## Deliverables
- Vector persistence layer and nearest-neighbor query path
- Upsert/update protocol tied to chunk identity/version
- Retention-aware cleanup strategy (delete/tombstone/rebuild)
- Consistency checks between vector and lexical stores

## Why this matters
Hybrid search fails if vector and lexical stores drift or lifecycle behavior is undefined.

## Acceptance
- Vector retrieval is deterministic for fixed index/model versions
- Lifecycle operations preserve referential integrity to recorder offsets
- Drift detection mechanisms are implemented and testable","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MistyCompass","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.341213Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-14T01:14:41.077988Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"labels":["epic","flight-recorder","track-semantic","vector"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:47.152699Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.341213Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.631779Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.687594Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":135,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.3","author":"AmberWolf","text":"AmberWolf claiming wa-oegrb.5.3 after closing wa-oegrb.5.2. Next slice: wire new chunking policy output into semantic/vector lifecycle so chunk identity/version drives deterministic upsert/update behavior and retention-safe drift checks between lexical and vector stores. I will reserve touched files before edits and post progress updates via Agent Mail thread wa-oegrb.5.3.","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:41:57Z"},{"id":152,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.3","author":"MistyCompass","text":"Taking over continuation from latest checkpoint to unblock wa-oegrb.5.4. Scope: vector lifecycle integrity (upsert/update/delete), retention-aware cleanup, and drift checks between segment_embeddings and lexical store; will validate with focused storage tests and report evidence in this thread.","created_at":"2026-02-14T01:14:41Z"}]}
-{"id":"wa-oegrb.5.4","title":"Implement explainable hybrid rank fusion with lexical-safe fallback semantics","description":"## Context
User goal is hybrid lexical/semantic search. We need explicit rank-fusion logic (not ad-hoc mixing) that is explainable and tunable.

## Scope
Implement hybrid ranker (e.g., RRF/weighted fusion), including fallback semantics and score diagnostics.

## Deliverables
- Rank fusion implementation with configurable weights/strategy
- Deterministic fallback to lexical-only when semantic path degraded
- Debug/explain output exposing component score contributions
- Tests validating stable ordering under representative queries

## Why this matters
Explainable, deterministic fusion is required for operator trust and practical debugging.

## Acceptance
- Hybrid ranking improves agreed quality metrics over lexical baseline
- Fallback behavior prevents semantic outages from breaking search
- Score diagnostics allow rapid triage of ranking anomalies","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.386875Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.802755Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"labels":["epic","flight-recorder","ranking","track-semantic"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.802620Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.386875Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.741484Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"wa-oegrb.5.5","title":"Create semantic/hybrid evaluation harness with measurable quality gates","description":"## Context
Semantic systems require ongoing evaluation to prevent regressions when models/chunking/ranker settings evolve.

## Scope
Build semantic+hybrid quality harness with curated forensic/operator queries and expected retrieval behavior.

## Deliverables
- Evaluation corpus and judgment rubric (precision/recall/NDCG-style)
- Baseline comparisons: lexical vs semantic vs hybrid
- Regression thresholds and reporting format
- Repeatable benchmark tooling integrated into CI or nightly runs

## Why this matters
Without explicit evaluation gates, semantic changes can silently degrade real-world utility.

## Acceptance
- Harness yields reproducible metrics across runs
- Hybrid improvements (or tradeoffs) are quantified, not anecdotal
- Regression thresholds are enforced in automation","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.433931Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.861817Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"labels":["epic","flight-recorder","testing","track-semantic"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.433931Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.861758Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.5.6","title":"Enforce semantic latency/cost budgets with caching and adaptive controls","description":"## Context
Embedding generation and vector retrieval introduce cost and latency risks. Budgets/caching are required for sustainable always-on operation.

## Scope
Define and implement latency/cost budget controls (batching, caching, adaptive refresh) for semantic path.

## Deliverables
- Budget targets and guardrails (p95 latency, per-day compute cost)
- Cache design for embeddings/query results with invalidation policy
- Rate-limit/backpressure behavior for embedding pipeline
- Telemetry dashboards for cost/latency tracking

## Why this matters
A high-quality semantic system that is too slow or expensive cannot be kept enabled in production.

## Acceptance
- Semantic path stays within defined cost/latency envelopes under representative load
- Cache invalidation is correct and tested
- Operators can monitor budget consumption in near real time","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.511574Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.908675Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"labels":["epic","flight-recorder","operations","performance","track-semantic"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.511574Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.908622Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.6","title":"Interface track: CLI/robot/MCP query surfaces for recorder + hybrid search","description":"## Track objective
Expose the new recorder/search capabilities through consistent human CLI, robot mode, and MCP interfaces with stable contracts.

## Why this track exists
Without usable interfaces, core platform improvements remain inaccessible to operators and agents.

## Deliverables
- CLI command extensions for flight-recorder queries/replay
- Robot-mode schema additions for lexical/semantic/hybrid search control
- MCP resources/tools for transcript retrieval and hybrid querying
- Saved queries and reproducible query payload support

## Acceptance criteria
- Interface contracts are documented and validated by contract tests.
